title: Runbook: Rolling Back a Serving Deploy
bodyMarkdown:
How to roll back a bad serving deploy, regardless of whether it went out blue-green or canary.

## When to roll back
Any sustained rise in error rate or latency after a deploy, or a config validation failure reported by src/serving/configValidate.ts that the deploy pipeline did not catch pre-rollout. Do not wait for a full incident review to decide, roll back first and investigate after traffic is healthy.

## Rollback steps
Revert the deploy through the same pipeline that shipped it, targeting the last known-good replica set. For a canary rollout, this means setting the canary weight in src/serving/router.ts back to zero and letting stable replicas absorb all traffic before tearing the canary set down. For a blue-green rollout, this means flipping the router back to the previous color.

## Verifying the rollback
Watch the metrics exporter dashboard for error rate and p99 latency to return to baseline, usually within a few minutes. Confirm no replica from the bad deploy is still receiving traffic.

## Afterward
File an incident if customer-facing impact occurred. Elena Petrova owns this runbook and SRE on-call generally, page her rotation for anything unclear.
